page: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor. This x86_64 architecture instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz and is supported by 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for deep learning operations. It does not include local storage or a GPU, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core scoring in the top 25% at 1892.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 32.91 GB/sec. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth present notable tradeoffs. The multi-core Geekbench score of 4435.0 and stress-ng multi-core performance of 3944.96 fall into the bottom 25%. Memory read bandwidth is also limited, with uncached large block reads measured at 26.17 GB/sec. Static web serving throughput is moderate at 7.35 GB/sec, while database operations and Redis SET performance remain in the lower 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i-flex.xlarge provides access to modern Intel Xeon architecture and DDR5 memory without the premium cost of fully-scaled general-purpose instances, making it a viable option for workloads that do not continuously saturate multi-core resources. The instance is best suited for single-threaded applications, basic web serving, light text processing, and small-scale language model text generation. It is less optimal for memory-bound databases, multi-threaded software compilation, or heavy parallel computing tasks due to its lower multi-core efficiency and lack of local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz under the Nitro hypervisor, it supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core execution, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are weaker, placing in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient entry to Gen7 Intel architecture, making it suitable for single-threaded applications, light web serving, and text processing, while being less optimal for memory-intensive databases or highly parallelized workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance, balanced by moderate multi-core and network capabilities.

tagline: A general-purpose Intel Xeon instance balancing strong single-core performance and DDR5 memory for standard workloads without local storage.
